summary: Atwood recounts debugging a strange duplicate-record bug that turned out to be caused by users double-clicking an OK button. This leads him to a broader critique of double-click as a UI paradigm, arguing it was a workaround forced by Apple's single-button mouse in 1984 that has persisted despite being unintuitive and difficult to execute. He notes that many users never learn the distinction between single and double-click, so they defensively double-click everything, causing problems across applications. The web's single-click model is presented as the superior approach, and Atwood calls for the death of double-click as a standard interaction pattern.
key_points: A production bug was caused by users double-clicking a button that developers assumed would only be single-clicked, illustrating the gap between developer and user mental models
Double-click originated as a workaround for Apple's single-button mouse limitation, not as an inherently good design choice
The mechanics of double-clicking are demanding—requiring precise timing and motor control—making it an unnecessarily difficult gesture for such a common action
Many users never fully learn when to single vs double-click, so they double-click everything defensively, poisoning their behavior across all applications
The web's single-click model avoids these problems and should be the standard going forward
money_quotes: No developer would ever double-click on a button, because developers have too much GUI knowledge.
Many users never fully learn the distinction between single-click and double-click. So they give up and simply... double-click everything.
Double-click has always been a bad idea.
How would anyone know, without being told, that double-click is a possible action? It's a completely hidden behavior.
Can't we just put another damn button on the mouse? Is two buttons really that hard to figure out?
Double-click is such a profoundly bad idea that it actually manages to spill over into other applications and poison them with bad learned user behavior.
key_insight: Double-click is a legacy workaround from 1984's single-button mouse limitation that persists despite being unintuitive, mechanically demanding, and responsible for poisoning user behavior across all applications.
